text.title: Rfc Lite Pm Template Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Rfc Lite Pm Template. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: RFC-lite has exactly four sections: problem, evidence, scope, open questions.
rule.rationale: RFC-lite has exactly four sections: problem, evidence, scope, open questions.

rule.statement: RFC names ONE accountable owner + ONE reviewer; 'team' / 'we' are forbidden.
rule.rationale: RFC names ONE accountable owner + ONE reviewer; 'team' / 'we' are forbidden.

rule.statement: Every claim in 'problem' or 'scope' cites an evidence anchor (URL / quote / metric).
rule.rationale: Every claim in 'problem' or 'scope' cites an evidence anchor (URL / quote / metric).

rule.statement: RFC links to the outcome ID it serves; otherwise it's a feature without a goal.
rule.rationale: RFC links to the outcome ID it serves; otherwise it's a feature without a goal.

rule.statement: RFC schedules a review date (default: 30 days post-ship) on the calendar.
rule.rationale: RFC schedules a review date (default: 30 days post-ship) on the calendar.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-rfc-lite-pm-template.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
